summary.sosovalue: Community sentiment around SoSoValue during April 12-13, 2026, appears predominantly positive yet mixed with anticipation and minor concerns, primarily driven by discussions on Season 2 EXP airdrop farming, bot screening processes, and high EXP thresholds observed in leaderboards (e.g., accounts over 100M EXP while others struggle at 10M). Key conversations revolve around app usage tutorials, trading volume achievements for airdrop eligibility (e.g., users completing 120% S2 progress with $100k volume), accidental vault withdrawals and their impact on claims, and promotional shares linking to SoSoValue watchlists or market insights like SSI Layer1 index drops. Metrics indicate low to moderate engagement overall, with most posts garnering 0-2 likes and under 300 views, though one tutorial post hit 39 likes and 2345 views, reflecting niche but active farming community interest amid upcoming snapshots; no widespread negativity, but frustration over EXP disparities and verification boosts (e.g., 120% verification) highlights competitive dynamics.
summary.sodex: Sentiment for Sodex is optimistic and instructional, focusing on mainnet trading boosts (5% SoPoints bonus), new features like Link Mobile Device to reduce cross-device friction, and API closed alpha for automated bots/AI trading, appealing to quants and developers requiring Silver tier, $10k account value, and $300k volume. Discussions emphasize SoPoints weekly 1M rewards, referral farming, and building on the platform (e.g., trading bots, dashboards), with users sharing guides in multiple languages and high-volume flexes; a standout post on mobile linking garnered 198 likes, 37 reposts, and 10k+ views, signaling growing utility adoption. Engagement metrics are higher here, peaking at 198 likes, but many posts remain low-volume (0-10 likes), indicating enthusiastic early adopters amid testnet appeal resolutions.
summary.ssi: Mentions of SSI (likely SSI Index or ssiLayer1) are sparse with neutral to slightly bearish tones, including a Japanese post noting a 3% 24-hour drop in ssiLayer1 index linked to SoSoValue data and unrelated crypto indices like MSSI (systemic stress at 64.3 high) and MSXI sentiment; no dedicated high-engagement discussions, primarily incidental in broader market fragility talks. Key points touch on volatility, stress indicators for BTC/ETH, but lack project-specific hype or questions. Metrics show minimal traction: 0 likes/views on the index drop post, 3 likes on MSSI explanations, underscoring low visibility and no significant community buzz during the period.
